conjecture_title: Determine the order of f_k(N; s, t) in general
paper_title: Independent sets in hypergraphs with a forbidden link
summary: The source paper (Fox–He, 2021) resolves the Erdős–Hajnal threshold conjectures and establishes $f_3(N;s,t)=\Theta(\log N/\log\log N)$ for a constant fraction of $t$-values in the range $(e(s),E(s)]$ for each fixed $s\ge 4$, but the full problem of determining the order of $f_k(N;s,t)$ for all valid parameters $k\ge 3$, $s\ge k+1$, and $1\le t\le\binom{s}{k}$ remains open. A wide web search through May 2026 found no follow-up paper settling the general question; the problem is still active.
search_queries: Fox He "independent sets hypergraphs forbidden link" f_k(N;s,t) order arXiv
hypergraph independent sets "forbidden link" "Erdos-Hajnal" threshold phase transition 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: No follow-up resolving the general order of f_k(N;s,t) was found. The 2026 preprint arXiv:2603.16069 (He) concerns quasipolynomial Ramsey numbers for 3-graphs but does not address the f_k(N;s,t) order problem. A Cambridge Core paper on forbidden order-size pairs is thematically related but uses a different framework and does not resolve this conjecture.
